sources.notes: Used to confirm the alternating-distance radius formula, interval intersection test, and greedy prefix partition proof.

reference_candidates.notes: 洛谷题解候选来源；只有实际参考后才移动到 sources。

annotation.summary: Given sorted disk centers on a line, choose positive radii to maximize how many adjacent disks can be tangent without invalid overlaps.
annotation.constraints: The point sequence is large enough that only a linear scan over consecutive distances is acceptable.
annotation.core_idea: For a connected block, let the first radius be x. Tangency forces every later radius to be an alternating sum of adjacent distances plus or minus x. The positivity and non-overlap constraints become open intervals of feasible x; maintaining the intersection tells how far the current block can extend. When the intersection becomes empty, the previous block is maximal and a greedy restart is optimal because starting the next block earlier cannot extend beyond that maximal prefix.
annotation.complexity: O(n) per test case after reading the points.

solution_variants.name: main
solution_variants.summary: Scan adjacent gaps, maintain the current valid interval for the first radius of the active block, and cut the block exactly when the interval becomes empty.

tags.evidence: A connected prefix is feasible exactly when all derived intervals for the first radius have nonempty intersection.
tags.description: Reduce feasibility to maintaining an intersection of numeric intervals for one hidden parameter.
tags.created_reason: The core of this geometry problem is not a standard data structure but a reusable interval-intersection feasibility pattern.

tags.evidence: The constraints are derived from tangency and non-overlap of disks centered on a line.
The solution repeatedly takes the largest feasible prefix and proves that restarting at the next index is optimal.
Each radius condition becomes a pair of strict inequalities on the first radius x.
The farthest feasible endpoint function is monotone enough for the maximal-prefix greedy proof.
